perturbation.original_text: 1.20 GIE Fees. The fees due to either PHLVIC or PLIC, as applicable, by a Certificate Owner under a Certificate for coverage under such Certificate.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.20 GIE Fees. The fees due to either PHLVIC or PLIC, as applicable, by a Certificate Owner under a Certificate.
perturbation.explanation: The removal of 'for coverage under such Certificate' from the definition of GIE Fees in Section 1.20 creates an in-text contradiction. While the contract refers to GIE Fees throughout, the original text specifies GIE Fees are for coverage under a Certificate. Now the term is ambiguous. For instance, Section A.1 of Exhibit A, says 'Notifying ICC of any change to the GIE Fee rate at least ten calendar days prior to the effective date of the fee change via E-mail'. The meaning of this notification is uncertain. This change introduces uncertainty about what exactly the GIE Fees cover, potentially leading to disputes over charges and coverage.
perturbation.justification: The removal of 'for coverage under such Certificate' from the definition of GIE Fees in Section 1.20 creates an in-text contradiction. The original text specifies GIE Fees are for coverage under a Certificate. Now the term is ambiguous. The contradicted text, Section A.1 of Exhibit A, states 'Notifying ICC of any change to the GIE Fee rate at least ten calendar days prior to the effective date of the fee change via E-mail'. Since the change introduces uncertainty about what the GIE Fees cover, this section is contradicted because it's unclear what rate change should be notified, potentially leading to disputes. This aligns with the definition of Omissions - In Text Contradiction, as essential language was removed creating a contradiction with obligations stated in other sections.

perturbation.original_text: 1.35 Master Group Annuity Contracts. The Master Group Annuity Contracts entered into by and between PHLVIC and ICC or an Affiliate, and by and between PLIC and ICC or an Affiliate
perturbation.changed_text: 1.35 Master Group Annuity Contracts. The Master Group Annuity Contracts entered into by and between PHLVIC and ICC or an Affiliate
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase 'and by and between PLIC and ICC or an Affiliate' from the definition of Master Group Annuity Contracts in Section 1.35, there is now a contradiction within the contract. Section 3.10 originally stated 'ICC shall cause itself or an Affiliate to enter into the Master Group Annuity Contracts by and between PHLVIC and ICC or an Affiliate, and by and between PLIC and ICC or an Affiliate'. The omission of the phrase in Section 1.35 creates confusion about whether a Master Group Annuity Contract involves PLIC or solely PHLVIC, affecting the enforceability of obligations related to PLIC.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.10 Master Group Annuity Contracts. ICC shall cause itself or an Affiliate to enter into the Master Group Annuity Contracts by and between PHLVIC and ICC or an Affiliate, and by and between PLIC and ICC or an Affiliate
perturbation.justification: The omission of 'and by and between PLIC and ICC or an Affiliate' from the definition of Master Group Annuity Contracts in Section 1.35 directly contradicts Section 3.10, which explicitly states that the Master Group Annuity Contracts are entered into by both PHLVIC and PLIC with ICC or an Affiliate. This omission creates a conflict regarding which entities are involved in the Master Group Annuity Contracts, making it unclear if PLIC is still a party, thus impacting enforceability related to PLIC's obligations. The change alters a definition in a way that breaks consistency with the contradicted section.

perturbation.original_text: 1.54 Territory. The Territory shall initially consist of all States, as may be changed from time to time by the written agreement of the Parties.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.54 Territory. The Territory shall initially consist of all States
perturbation.explanation: The omission of 'as may be changed from time to time by the written agreement of the Parties' from the definition of 'Territory' creates an in-text contradiction, because it implies the territory cannot be changed. This contradicts Section 7.02 where it says 'PHL Variable shall take all commercially reasonable efforts to (1) qualify the offer and sale of the GIE in each jurisdiction within the Territory'. Without a mention that the territory may change, this obligation is not fully clear about it's scope and how it may evolve.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7.02 GIE Filing and Approval. PHL Variable shall take all commercially reasonable efforts to (1) qualify the offer and sale of the GIE in each jurisdiction within the Territory, and (2) obtain any approvals that are or may be required by any Governmental Entity to permit or facilitate the offer and sale of the GIE in each such jurisdiction; provided however that neither PHL Variable nor any other PHL Party shall be required to seek to qualify the offer and sale of the GIE or obtain any approvals to permit or facilitate the offer or sale of the GIE in the State of New York or the State of Maine. Such actions shall include, as applicable, filing the Registration Statement and using commercially reasonable efforts to maintain the effectiveness of the Registration Statement, filing forms of the Master Group Annuity Contracts, Certificates, and Applications with Governmental Entities, - 13 - including State insurance departments, and filing or submitting such notices, requests, and other documents in furtherance of the foregoing. One or more of the PHL Parties will immediately notify ICC if any Governmental Entity withdraws or modifies a previously issued approval of the GIE.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed for the Territory to be changed via written agreement. The changed text removes this possibility, creating a conflict with Section 7.02, which implies an ongoing obligation to qualify the GIE in each jurisdiction within the Territory. By removing the possibility of territory changes, the obligation in Section 7.02 becomes unclear and potentially unenforceable if the territory cannot be changed according to the changed text in Section 1.54.
